(S05) 46tH3600 
FIRE 
6005 LEWIS AVENUE 
ATASCADERO, 
(805) 466-2141 
I would like to request your opinion as to whether the following factual 
situation constitutes a valid conflict of interest. 
The City of Atascadero is a general law city, having five council members, 
including a council-selected mayor. 
The City's offices are located in the 
Veterans Memorial Building, which was constructed many years ago by the 
developer of Atascadero. 
A number of charitable and County offices are 
located in the Veterans Memorial Building, now sometimes called City Hall. 
Thus, the County rents space from the City for a very reasonable rent. 
Part 
of the space that is rented by the County is for the location of the adult 
and children's library in the building. 
One of the council members is em-
ployed by the County as a part-time librarian in the County library located 
in the City Hall building. 
On several occasions, the question has arisen as to whether the County library 
should be relocated to a site other than the City Hall building so that the 
space can be used for city space, such as for renovation and expansion of the 
Police Department and/or other city uses. 
The question arises as to whether there is any impediment as to this council 
member voting on an issue that affects and involves her employer's agency, 
to wit, the County library. 
Thank you for your assistance. 
Very truly yours, 
ALLEN GRIMES 
City Attorney 
AG:fr 
cc: 
Murray Warden, City Manager

sections.question: You asked whether there would be a conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act, Sections 87100, et seq., for the council member/librarian to participate in council decisions involving the use of the City's space by the County Library. These decisions include resolving whether or not the County Library should be relocated so that the space can be used for City uses.
sections.conclusion: Based upon the facts given, I conclude that the council member does not have a conflict of interest with respect to these decisions. Since the County Library is a local government agency, the salary received by the councilmember is not 'income,' and thus the County is not a 'source of income' to her. Accordingly, the councilmember does not have to refrain from making or participating in decisions involving the County Library when the only connection between the councilmember and the County Library is the employment relationship.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a city council member employed part-time by the County as a librarian has a conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act when voting on decisions regarding the County Library's use of space in City Hall, including potential relocation.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concludes no conflict of interest exists because salary from a local government agency is statutorily excluded from the definition of 'income' under the Political Reform Act, so the County Library employment does not create a disqualifying financial interest.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both incoming request letter (December 31, 1981) and FPPC response letter (January 26, 1982). Extracted information from FPPC response. Some text degradation in OCR but key sections are legible. Question and conclusion sections clearly identified and extracted verbatim from FPPC response.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: You asked whether there would be a conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act, Sections 87100, et seq., for the council member/librarian to participate in council decisions involving the use of the City's space by the County Library. These decisions include resolving whether or not the County Library should be relocated so that the space can be used for City uses.

CONCLUSION: Based upon the facts given, I conclude that the council member does not have a conflict of interest with respect to these decisions. Since the County Library is a local government agency, the salary received by the councilmember is not 'income,' and thus the County is not a 'source of income' to her. Accordingly, the councilmember does not have to refrain from making or participating in decisions involving the County Library when the only connection between the councilmember and the County Library is the employment relationship.

embedding.summary: The FPPC advised City Attorney Allen Grimes that a city council member employed part-time by the County as a librarian may participate in council votes regarding the County Library's space in City Hall without a conflict of interest, as government employment income is statutorily excluded from the Act's definition of income.
